evals.prompt: The scope is approved: migrate the API layer, update the client hooks, and refresh the docs. Orchestrate this across parallel workers.
evals.expected_output: Scope decomposed into slices meeting the runnable-slice contract, plan presented for approval, then worktrees created and workers dispatched.

assertions.text: Presents the slice plan to the user and waits for approval before dispatching any worker
Each slice includes the full runnable-slice contract, including objectively checkable acceptance criteria
The slice plan specifies an integration branch and one pre-created worktree with a slice branch per worker before dispatch
The slice plan states the concurrency cap and queues slices beyond it
Does not implement any slice itself

evals.prompt: Worker 2 messaged: the assigned refactor would change public API behavior, should it proceed with a breaking change or add a compatibility layer?
evals.expected_output: Classified as needing human approval; surfaced to the user, decision relayed back as the confirmed next action; the worker is not left blocked.

assertions.text: Classifies the escalation and surfaces the scope-affecting decision to the user rather than deciding unilaterally
Records the escalation and leaves the worker relay pending until the user supplies a decision, without inventing a confirmed action

evals.prompt: Worker 4 has been silent for a long time while the other three finished their slices.
evals.expected_output: Staleness handling per stop conditions: one status ping, then treat as dead, salvage committed work, respawn with a tightened assignment.

assertions.text: Sends one status ping before declaring the worker dead
Salvages committed work from the slice branch and respawns with a tightened assignment rather than waiting indefinitely

evals.prompt: All slices are accepted. Integrate and deliver.
evals.expected_output: Slice branches merged one at a time into the integration branch with project-wide verification after each, one final PR, worktrees cleaned up.

assertions.text: Merges or cherry-picks one slice branch at a time and runs project-wide verification after each merge
Opens exactly one final PR from the integration branch and never commits directly to main
Removes slice worktrees and deletes merged slice branches

evals.prompt: A worker's third fix-up round still fails the acceptance criteria.
evals.expected_output: The fix-up cap triggers: the slice stops and escalates to the user with diffs and evidence instead of a fourth round.

assertions.text: Stops the slice at the fix-up cap and escalates to the user with the diffs and evidence

evals.prompt: Implement this small validation bug fix for me.
evals.expected_output: swarm does not engage for a single solo slice; blaze or direct work applies.

assertions.text: Does not spin up coordination, worktrees, or workers for a single small task
